tenders.title: Independent Engineer Services during Operation & Maintenance of 4-laning of Jind–Gohana–Sonepat Section (Km 0.000–78.837) of NH-352A, Haryana (HAM)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee not disclosed in RFP (IE fee to be quoted)
tenders.scopeSummary: Independent Engineer services during the O&M period of the 78.837 km 4-laned Jind–Gohana–Sonepat section of NH-352A under Bharatmala Pariyojana on Hybrid Annuity Mode. The IE supervises operation, maintenance and safety compliance on behalf of NHAI. RFP downloadable 29.01.2026–02.03.2026; bid due 02.03.2026.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience as IE/AE/Supervision Consultant on 2 construction projects of 4/6-laning of 30% of project length each, or one project of 50% of project length, in last 7 years; plus O&M/DPR experience per data sheet

No debarment/penalty by MoRTH or agencies in last 2 years

tenders.title: MoRTH Model Request for Proposal (RFP) for Consultancy Services — QCBS
tenders.scopeSummary: MoRTH model RFP that road authorities adapt for consultancy assignments. Defines the Tender Information Summary (TIS), consultant forms (T-2 organisation & experience, T-2A technical capacity, T-3 comments on ToR, T-4 approach/methodology/work plan, T-5 work schedule, T-6 team composition & CVs), Section VII scoring criteria, QCBS as the default selection method, and mandatory INFRACON registration with a unique Team ID.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Defined per assignment in TIS; typically similar-nature consultancy in last 5–7 years
keyPersonnel: Key Experts defined per assignment ToR
other: Mandatory INFRACON registration of firm and all key personnel
Unique INFRACON Team ID quoted with bid
Bid via eprocure.gov.in

technicalCriteria.criterion: Consultant's relevant experience & past performance (Form T-2/T-2A)
technicalCriteria.notes: Weight set per assignment in Section VII

technicalCriteria.criterion: Approach, methodology & work plan (Form T-4)
Qualifications & experience of Key Experts (Form T-6 + CVs)
technicalCriteria.notes: Usually the largest weight; Team Leader gate common

tenders.sourceDocument: MoRTH Model RFP for Consultancy Services (QCBS)

tenders.title: PMGSY Quality Monitoring & Mandatory Road Safety Audit (Rural Roads)
tenders.issuingAuthority: NRIDA (National Rural Infrastructure Development Agency), Ministry of Rural Development
tenders.scopeSummary: PMGSY operates a three-tier quality management mechanism; the third tier is independent National Quality Monitors (NQMs) — empanelled retired senior engineers engaged by NRIDA to inspect and test rural road works on a random-sampling basis. Additionally, a Road Safety Audit is mandatory at the design stage for all PMGSY-III roads longer than 5 km, creating demand for road-safety-audit consultancies.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: NQM empanelment requires senior engineering experience (State/Central organisations); road safety audit requires certified safety auditors
other: Empanelment model (individuals) rather than firm QCBS for NQMs
Road Safety Audit mandatory for PMGSY-III roads >5 km per Supreme Court Committee on Road Safety

technicalCriteria.criterion: Engineering seniority & domain experience (NQM empanelment)
Road safety audit certification & experience

tenders.sourceDocument: NRIDA/PMGSY Guidelines on Quality Assurance & Control Mechanism; Performance Evaluation of NQMs

evaluationFramework.summary:
NHAI Independent Engineer / Authority Engineer selection is governed by Policy Circular No. 11.99 (17.07.2026), Section VII. Technical proposals are scored out of 100 marks across four criteria: Relevant experience for the assignment (25), Rating of the Firm as published by NHAI (40), Experience in use of technology for road inspection (10), and Qualifications & competence of key staff (25).

A technical proposal must typically score at least 75/100 to be responsive for financial evaluation (Independent Engineer / BOT-HAM variant); Authority Engineer / EPC variants often use 70/100. Bid weightages under the circular are Technical proposal 60%, Performance security quoted by bidder 20%, and Financial quote 20%. Bidders must self-evaluate against Appendix-EC with documentary proof on INFRACON; detailed evaluation of a bidder is shared with that bidder only (7 days to respond to objections).

Firm experience credits average consultancy turnover vs total project cost (TPC), highway professionals on payroll, IE/AE/supervision and DPR counts relative to project length (last 7 years), and major-structure (>500 m) work. Firm rating uses NHAI’s published rating bands (e.g. ≥80 → 40 marks; <55 → 0), with JV weighted averages. Technology marks require ownership (or JV experience certificates) for NSV, Mobile QC unit, NABL lab, MBIU, FWD and retro-reflectometer. Key staff (25 marks) are position-weighted (e.g. Team Leader 7 on normal highway projects) with each CV scored on qualification, adequacy, employment with firm and age; Team Leader gates and medical-certificate rules from Appendix-B apply. Residual AE/IE bid capacity R = CL × TF − RP must exceed project length.

criteria.label: Relevant Experience for the Assignment
criteria.description: NHAI Circular 11.99 — 25 marks. Covers average annual consultancy turnover (last 3 years) vs TPC, number of highway professionals (≥8 years, >1 year with firm), IE/AE/supervision/PMC highway projects of similar category/length (last 7 years), DPR highway projects (≥40% length), and AE/IE/PMC or DPR of major structures >500 m. JV lead + partner experience may be aggregated. AE/IE work for private clients is not counted. Length factors: for 4/6-laning bids, 2-lane experience ×0.4 (only if consultancy cost >₹3 Cr); for 2-lane bids, 4/6-lane experience ×1.5.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Near-full sub-marks: turnover well above 3% TPC, >30 highway professionals, multiple completed IE/AE assignments of project length, DPRs + major structures (>500 m) with proof
Meets most Appendix-EC bands: turnover ~3%+ TPC, 20–30 professionals, 1–2 similar IE/AE projects, some DPR/structure credits
Clears floors with thin margins: turnover near 3% TPC, 10–20 professionals, minimal project counts at band minimums
Below key floors (turnover <3% TPC, <10 professionals, or insufficient similar-length IE/AE/DPR) — little or no credit

inputFields.label: Avg annual consultancy turnover (last 3 yrs) as % of this project's TPC
inputFields.scoring: Max 5: <3% TPC = 0; 3% = 3.0; +0.5 per extra 1% TPC up to +2.0 (cap 5)

inputFields.label: Highway professionals with the firm (Civil/Transport/Traffic/Geology/Env, ≥8 yrs; employed >1 yr)
IE/AE/Construction Supervision/PMC highway projects of project length & similar category (last 7 yrs)
inputFields.scoring: Max 7: 1 project = 3; +0.5 per completed assignment up to +4

inputFields.label: DPR preparation — highway projects ≥40% of project length, similar category (last 7 yrs)
inputFields.scoring: Max 3: 2 projects = 2; +0.5 each additional up to +1

inputFields.label: AE/IE/PMC construction supervision of major structures >500 m (last 7 yrs)
inputFields.scoring: Max 3: 2 projects = 1.0; +0.5 each additional up to +2.0

inputFields.label: DPR of major structures >500 m (last 7 yrs)
inputFields.scoring: Max 2: 2 projects = 1.0; +0.5 each additional up to +1.0

criteria.label: Rating of the Firm (NHAI Published)
criteria.description: NHAI Circular 11.99 — 40 marks. Uses the firm's NHAI-published rating. JV uses weighted average S1×R1 + S2×R2 (lead × other partner shares). For tentative project value ≤₹500 Cr, new entrants in JV with no historical rating may use only the other partner's rating. Non-substantial JV partners are ignored.

inputFields.label: Your firm's NHAI published rating (0–100)
Bidding as a Joint Venture?
inputFields.scoring: If Yes, use weighted average of partner ratings (S1×R1 + S2×R2)

inputFields.label: If JV — lead partner share (%)
inputFields.scoring: S1 in weighted average rating formula

inputFields.label: If JV — other substantial partner's NHAI rating
inputFields.scoring: R2; non-substantial partner rating not considered

criteria.label: Technology for Road Inspection
criteria.description: NHAI Circular 11.99 — 10 marks for ownership (with proof) of inspection technology: Network Survey Vehicle (2.0), Mobile Quality Control Unit with soil/asphalt/concrete/aggregate/pavement testing + IT (3.0), NABL Accredited Lab (1.5), Mobile Bridge Inspection Unit or better (1.5), Falling Weight Deflectometer or better (1.0), Retro-reflectometer (1.0). JV firm experience also counted with experience certificates (Govt-certified if for private concessionaire).

scoringGuidance.meaning: Owns essentially the full equipment stack (NSV + Mobile QC + NABL + MBIU + FWD + retro) with ownership proof
Owns most high-weight items (e.g. Mobile QC + NSV + one of MBIU/FWD/NABL)
Owns 2–3 items or relies partly on certified JV experience
Little or no owned equipment / unverified experience

inputFields.label: Own Network Survey Vehicle (NSV) technology?
inputFields.scoring: 2.0 marks with ownership proof

inputFields.label: Own Mobile Quality Control Unit (soil/asphalt/concrete/aggregate/pavement + IT)?
inputFields.scoring: 3.0 marks

inputFields.label: Own NABL Accredited Lab?
inputFields.scoring: 1.5 marks

inputFields.label: Own Mobile Bridge Inspection Unit (MBIU) or better?
inputFields.scoring: 1.5 marks

inputFields.label: Own Falling Weight Deflectometer (FWD) or better?
inputFields.scoring: 1.0 mark

inputFields.label: Own Retro-reflectometer technology?
inputFields.scoring: 1.0 mark

criteria.label: Qualifications & Competence of Key Staff
criteria.description: NHAI Circular 11.99 — 25 marks. Normal highway project position weights: Team Leader cum Senior Highway Engineer (7), Resident cum Highway Engineer (4), Bridge/Structural Engineer (3), Pavement Specialist (3), Quality cum Material Expert (3), Program Schedule Reviewer (2), Road Safety Expert (3). Standalone bridge/tunnel projects use alternate position sets. Each CV is scored /100 on general qualifications (~25%), adequacy for the project (~70%), and employment with firm (~5%), plus age limits where specified. Self-certified INFRACON undertakings without client certificates are not evaluated; CVs without medical fitness certificate (Appendix-B) score zero. Lower-category 'similar capacity' work is credited at 2/3.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Full key team proposed; TL and others clear Appendix-EC adequacy with strong similar-capacity EPC/HAM experience; employment & age favourable
All positions filled to circular weights; TL CV solidly above gate; most experts meet adequacy bands
Team mostly complete; TL marginal on adequacy/age; some positions weak or thinner similar-capacity proof
Missing key positions, TL fails gate / medical / essential PQ, or heavy reliance on lower-category similar capacity

inputFields.label: Project type (sets key-staff weight template)
inputFields.scoring: Selects Circular 11.99 key_staff_weightage_by_project_type table

inputFields.label: Team Leader CV self-score vs Appendix-EC (0–100%)
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 7 (highway); historically TL <75% can fail the proposal — verify RFP gate

inputFields.label: Can you field a Resident cum Highway Engineer meeting Circular 3.2?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 4 on normal highway projects

inputFields.label: Can you field a Bridge/Structural Engineer meeting Circular 3.3?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 3 (highway) / higher on bridge projects

inputFields.label: Can you field a Pavement Specialist meeting Circular 3.4?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 3 on normal highway projects

inputFields.label: Can you field a Quality cum Material Expert meeting Circular 3.5?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 3

inputFields.label: Can you field a Road Safety Expert meeting Circular 3.6?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 3

inputFields.label: Can you field a Program Schedule Reviewer meeting Circular 3.16?
inputFields.scoring: Position weight 2

inputFields.label: Firm + all key CVs on INFRACON with client certificates (not self-certified only)?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory for evaluation eligibility under Circular general notes

keyPolicies.name: NHAI Policy Circular No. 11.99 dated 17.07.2026 — Section VII Evaluation/Scoring Criteria
keyPolicies.relevance: Primary source for IE/AE technical scoring: 25/40/10/25 mark split, firm rating bands, technology ownership marks, key-staff weightages, and 60/20/20 bid weightages. Structured extract in research/policies/NHAI_Policy_Circular_11.99_Evaluation_Criteria.json.

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (MoF, Dept. of Expenditure, updated June 2022)
keyPolicies.relevance: Umbrella GFR consultancy selection systems (QCBS etc.) that NHAI RFPs implement alongside Circular 11.99.

keyPolicies.name: MoRTH Model RFP for Consultancy Services (QCBS)
keyPolicies.relevance: Sector RFP skeleton (TIS, Forms T-2–T-6, INFRACON Team ID) used with NHAI Appendix-EC scoring.

keyPolicies.name: INFRACON — National Portal for Infrastructure Consultancy Firms & Key Personnel
keyPolicies.relevance: Mandatory credential upload; Circular 11.99 refuses self-certified undertakings without client certificates.

keyPolicies.name: NRIDA/PMGSY — Guidelines on Quality Assurance & Control Mechanism
keyPolicies.relevance: Rural-roads quality regime distinct from NHAI IE/AE Circular 11.99 scoring.
